commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9720d05eda6f058a3911bb9ed417fa550c29a99e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin, Codex app-server, provider, manifest, native-approval, action-param, attachment, and cache/schema readers so malformed or unreadable metadata is quarantined, omitted, rejected, or treated as absent instead of throwing raw errors or widening decisions.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body gives direct before/after repros for unreadable synthetic metadata across provider, manifest, native approval, action-param, attachment, and JSON-param paths, and source inspection confirms those are the touched boundaries; I did not run tests locally because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A repair worker can address the two narrow schema/cache blockers, but final merge still needs maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed compatibility semantics.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, or SDK helper APIs.
- Kept test fixtures synthetic: current test content uses `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*` names rather than the reported third-party plugin name.
- Added follow-up hardening for Plugin SDK SSRF/private-network config reads, provider/config type-safety drift, Codex URL-backed image preservation, embedded-agent compaction schema-quarantine fixtures, provider wizard setup/model-picker metadata reads, manifest alias/channel reads used by gateway startup planning, native approval route/approver metadata reads, and shared action/tool param reader traps, and outbound structured attachment param traps, and outbound JSON param parser traps.
- Bound provider wizard setup/model-picker metadata reads so unreadable provider/method wizard fields cannot crash setup choices; readable sibling providers/methods still appear, onboarding scopes are copied without trusting array methods, and the provider receiver is preserved for model-selected hooks.
- Bound gateway startup manifest reads so unreadable manifest `plugins`, `channels`, `providers`, activation lists, contracts, setup provider ids, CLI backends, model-catalog keys, or legacy aliases cannot crash plugin id normalization or startup planning; readable sibling manifest metadata still resolves.
- Bound native approval helper reads so unreadable approver iterators, mapper callbacks, target field getters, or target matching hooks cannot crash approval forwarding; unreadable route identity fields fail closed instead of matching as absent.
- Bound shared action/tool param reads so unreadable snake/camel key probes, value getters, array entries, action gates, reaction remove flags, or boolean params cannot crash channel action handling; oversized string arrays now reject instead of silently truncating.
- Bound outbound structured attachment reads so unreadable attachment getters, iterators, and entries cannot crash media hint collection; oversized attachment arrays now reject instead of silently truncating.
- Bound outbound JSON param parsing for `presentation`, `delivery`, and `interactive` so unreadable getters, blank-value deletes, and parsed-value writes cannot throw raw proxy errors before message action normalization.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for an unreadable synthetic provider auth-method `wizard` getter: before fix it threw; after fix the readable `mockplugin` wizard option was returned.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic manifest `channels` metadata: before fix gateway startup planning threw inside plugin-id alias normalization; after fix readable `mockplugin` channel metadata was returned and unreadable `fuzzplugin` metadata was skipped.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic native approval approver iterators and identity fields: before fix approver iteration threw; after fix readable `mock-user-*` targets remain and unreadable route identity matches return false.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic action params: before fix proxy/getter traps threw raw errors from key lookup, reaction flags, and action gates; after fix required params become `ToolInputError`, optional params are absent, and gates fail closed.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic structured attachments: before fix attachment getter/iterator traps threw raw errors; after fix unreadable attachment payloads are absent and readable indexed entries remain available.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic JSON action params: before fix getter/delete traps threw raw errors; after fix unreadable params are absent, blank clearing is best-effort, invalid JSON keeps the normal parse error, and blocked writes report a normal update error.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/approval-native-helpers.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 14 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/common.params.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/boolean-param.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (2 files / 26 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 30 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 34 tests after JSON param parser hardening)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 109 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/plugin-registry.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 26 tests after latest rebase)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-wizard.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 10 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/ssrf-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 48 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/elevenlabs/speech-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 6 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (2 projects / 110 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/image-payload-sanitizer.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 7 tests)
- Focused affected batches for manifest/tool availability, provider auth/onboarding, channel/config fixtures, and fuzz guard type/lint drift (largest reruns: 11 files / 180 tests; 16 files / 323 tests; 8 files / 113 tests)
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` and `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the latest touched file sets
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Latest local autoreview on the native approval slice: clean after addressing its false-positive-match finding.
- Latest focused proof after rebase to `b240ce2085`: `approval-native-helpers.test.ts` passed 1 file / 14 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed. Latest post-rebase proof after rebase to `44dc29f397`: `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and final touched-tree fixture scrub passed.
- Prior branch autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings after rebasing onto then-current `origin/main`.
- Prior focused proof after rebase to `b0e9569ebd`: `channel-plugin-ids.test.ts` passed 1 file / 109 tests; `plugin-registry.test.ts` passed 1 file / 26 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the provider-wizard slice: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 on base `8e806e9125`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.
- Final rebase state: branch rebased onto `origin/main` at `d7fca5794d`; latest main drift had zero touched-path overlap with this branch.
- Current branch head after rebase/push: `9720d05eda6f058a3911bb9ed417fa550c29a99e`.
- Current test content scrub: no reported third-party plugin name remains in repo test/source/docs content checked for this branch.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization errors.
- Provider wizard setup choices skip unreadable provider/auth-method metadata while preserving readable sibling options and receiver-sensitive hooks.
- Gateway startup planning skips unreadable manifest alias/channel lists while preserving readable sibling channel ownership and plugin-id aliases.
- Native approval routing skips unreadable approver entries and fails closed when target identity cannot be read.
- Shared action/tool parameter readers turn unreadable required fields into normal input errors, treat unreadable optional fields as absent, fail unreadable action gates closed, and reject oversized string-array params instead of doing partial actions.
- Outbound structured attachment media hints copy by bounded index, skip unreadable entries, and reject oversized attachment arrays instead of crashing or partially truncating.
- Outbound JSON action params parse through guarded reads/writes so malformed plugin-provided accessors cannot throw before action normalization.
- Manifest tool availability remains fail-closed when config-signal metadata is unreadable or malformed.
- Valid URL-backed Codex image blocks remain preserved while invalid inline image payloads are still replaced with the existing omitted-image text.
- Embedded-agent schema-quarantine and plugin fixtures use synthetic names and pass focused proof.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.
- After the latest green AWS gate, `origin/main` advanced multiple times. Overlap at `9a7014ac38` was in `src/plugins/plugin-registry.test.ts`; the branch rebased cleanly and focused overlap proof passed. Later advances to `b0e9569ebd`, `b240ce2085`, `44dc29f397`, `1610b4983f`, `1211123fe6`, `36de671cad`, `30de7874cf`, `82cb02a4fd`, and `d7fca5794d` had zero touched-path overlap with the current slice where relevant; the branch rebased cleanly and focused proof was rerun for the latest touched surface.
- GitHub PR checks are expected to queue on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config or manifest inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid schema, provider hint, setup choice, channel allowlist, manifest alias, native approval route, or Codex payload field.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, keep valid URL-backed and inline image forms covered by tests, retain receiver-sensitive provider hooks, keep readable manifest aliases/channels resolving, fail closed on unreadable approval identity fields, and use focused regressions plus broad AWS `check:changed` gates across core and extension type/lint/guard lanes.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `9720d05eda6f058a3911bb9ed417fa550c29a99e`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. No local actionable autoreview findings remain.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed, including the Codex URL-backed image-block preservation regression, the provider wizard receiver regression found before commit, the native approval false-positive target match finding, and the shared param-reader silent-truncation finding.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
